trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline U-Net (7.8M params, base_ch=64) achieved Z500 RMSE=568.6, still improving at epoch 10. Analysis: Main bottlenecks are model capacity and training duration. 7.8M is tiny vs 1B limit. Decision: Scale model to base_ch=192 (~70M params), add SE attention and bottleneck self-attention, train 40 epochs with stride=1 (2x data), AdamW, warmup+cosine LR, channel-weighted loss (Z500 weight=2), longitude augmentation, EMA weights, and gradient clipping.

trajectory.description: Observation: train.py has a syntax error - 'def main():n' instead of 'def main():\n'. The file was corrupted. Analysis: Need to rewrite train.py completely with correct syntax, fixing both the newline issue and the device mismatch from iteration 2. Decision: Rewrite train.py from scratch with all improvements (base_ch=192, EMA, cosine LR, augmentation, channel-weighted loss) and correct device handling.

Observation: 80M model achieved Z500 RMSE=503.6 at epoch 8, then NaN from epoch 9 onward. Analysis: Mixed precision training caused numerical instability. The model was still improving rapidly when it diverged. Decision: Switch to fp32 training, lower LR to 5e-5, add strong gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0), add NaN detection. Keep base_ch=192 architecture but use stride=2 for faster epochs. Train 25 epochs first to verify stability, then scale up if stable.

trajectory.description: Observation: 80M U-Net achieved Z500 RMSE=498.8 at epoch 5, then overfit badly (538.3 by epoch 24). Training loss kept decreasing while val RMSE increased - classic overfitting. Analysis: Model has too much capacity for the training data size (stride=2 gives ~27k samples). No dropout, no lat-weighted training loss, and LR decayed to near-zero wasting epochs 15-24. Decision: 1) Add dropout=0.15 to model, 2) Use stride=1 (2x data), 3) Use lat-weighted MSE loss during training, 4) Increase LR to 3e-4 with proper warmup+cosine, 5) Add noise augmentation, 6) Early stopping with patience=15, 7) Save EMA weights as best checkpoint. Train for up to 60 epochs.

Observation: Training crashed at line 220 - device mismatch between residuals (CUDA) and last_inputs (CPU) in validation loop. The train.py was truncated so I can't see the exact bug, but the pattern is clear. Also, iteration 5 showed overfitting (best Z500 RMSE=498.8 at epoch 5, degraded to 538.3 by epoch 24). Analysis: Need to fix device handling in val loop by moving last_inputs to device before adding to residuals. Also need to address overfitting: stronger regularization, better LR schedule. Decision: Rewrite train.py with fixed device handling, keep stride=1 for more data, add stronger dropout, use cosine annealing with warmup properly, and train for 50 epochs with early stopping.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best Z500 RMSE=460.8 achieved at epoch 5 with base_ch=192 (80M params), then severe overfitting (491.6 by epoch 20). Training loss kept decreasing while val RMSE increased. Analysis: Model is too large for the dataset (54k samples), memorizing training data quickly. Key issue is overfitting, not underfitting. Decision: 1) Reduce model to base_ch=128 (~35M params) to reduce overfitting capacity, 2) Increase dropout to 0.25 and weight_decay to 0.1 for stronger regularization, 3) Add TTA (4 longitude shifts) in predict.py to reduce prediction variance, 4) Train for 35 epochs with patience=10. The smaller model should continue improving beyond epoch 5.

trajectory.description: Observation: Z500 RMSE stuck at ~459-461, model overfits by epoch 7 regardless of size (192→128 both peak early). Analysis: Overfitting is the bottleneck - training loss keeps decreasing while val RMSE increases. SWA (Stochastic Weight Averaging) can help by averaging weights across the overfitting trajectory, finding wider optima. Going smaller (base_ch=96) should slow overfitting further. Decision: Implement manual SWA starting at epoch 10 with constant LR, use base_ch=96, increase TTA from 4 to 8 shifts, z500_weight=4.0.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best Z500 RMSE = 460.9 (iter 8, base_ch=128, TTA=4). Iter 9 with base_ch=96+SWA got 483.2 (worse). Models overfit quickly (peak at epoch 5-7). Analysis: Single model is capped by overfitting. Ensemble of multiple models with different seeds is the most reliable way to improve. Adding EMA (exponential moving average) weights should also help generalization. Decision: Train 3 models with base_ch=128 (proven best size), different seeds, EMA, and ensemble their predictions with 8-shift TTA. This should reduce variance significantly.

Observation: Best Z500 RMSE = 460.9 (iter 8, single model base_ch=128). Ensemble of 3 models in iter 10 got 465.6 (worse because individual models were ~468-469 each, likely due to buggy EMA from truncated train.py). Models consistently overfit at epoch 4-5. Analysis: Need to improve single model quality. Key changes: 1) Add time derivative (diff = t - t-6h) as extra input channels (in_channels=8), providing explicit trend info. 2) Clean implementation without buggy EMA. 3) Lower LR (1e-4) with proper warmup+cosine to slow overfitting. 4) Delete old model_s*.pt files so predict.py uses only the new best model. 5) Use 4-shift TTA (0,16,32,48) which worked well in iter 8. Time budget: ~46 min remaining, training ~20 min for 20 epochs with stride=1.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best Z500 RMSE = 460.9 (iter 8, base_ch=128, in_channels=6, dropout=0.25, 4-shift TTA). Iter 11 with in_channels=8 (time derivative) got 469.1 val RMSE - time derivative hurt performance. Only ~22 min remaining. Analysis: The time derivative was not helpful. Need to revert to in_channels=6 (proven best) and try to improve further: 1) Increase TTA from 4 to 8 shifts for better prediction averaging, 2) Increase z500_weight to 4.0 since primary metric is rmse_z500, 3) Keep dropout=0.25 and base_ch=128 (best config). Decision: Retrain with in_channels=6, base_ch=128, dropout=0.25, z500_weight=4.0, max 15 epochs, and submit for eval. 8-shift TTA should reduce prediction variance and potentially beat 460.9.
